---
title: "The European Social Survey, round 11 - Trust"
format: 
  dashboard:
    theme:
      light: [litera, custom.scss]
      dark: [darkly, custom.scss]
    scrolling: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <link rel="icon" href="favicon.svg" type="image/svg+xml">
        <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
        <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Outfit:wght@400;500&family=Inter:wght@400;500&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
    nav-buttons:
      - icon: envelope
        href: mailto:teromeh@proton.me
      - icon: house-door
        href: ../
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
library(plotly)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

#   agea     - age of respondent (numeric)
#   ppltrst  - most people can be trusted (0-10)
#   pplfair  - most people try to be fair (0-10)
#   pplhlp   - most people try to be helpful (0-10)
#   trstprl  - trust in country's parliament (0-10)
#   trstlgl  - trust in the legal system (0-10)
#   trstplc  - trust in the police (0-10)
#   trstplt  - trust in politicians (0-10)
#   trstprt  - trust in political parties (0-10)
#   trstep   - trust in the European Parliament (0-10)
#   trstun   - trust in the United Nations (0-10)

data <- readRDS("ess-11-trust-all.rds")

title_font <- list(family = "Outfit, sans-serif", size = 14, color = "#1a1a1a")

ess_red <- "#ED3D42"

# Darker shade of red to highlight each country's most common trust score.
darken_hex <- function(hex, factor = 0.8) {
  rgb_vals <- col2rgb(hex) * factor
  rgb(rgb_vals[1], rgb_vals[2], rgb_vals[3], maxColorValue = 255)
}
ess_red_dark <- darken_hex(ess_red, factor = 0.8)

# Deeper maroon for hover tooltips - dark enough to keep white text legible,
# while still reading as part of the red palette.
ess_red_hover <- darken_hex(ess_red, factor = 0.45)

country_names <- c(
  AT = "Austria", BE = "Belgium", BG = "Bulgaria", HR = "Croatia", CY = "Cyprus",
  CZ = "Czechia", EE = "Estonia", FI = "Finland", FR = "France", DE = "Germany",
  GR = "Greece", HU = "Hungary", IS = "Iceland", IE = "Ireland", IL = "Israel",
  IT = "Italy", LV = "Latvia", LT = "Lithuania", ME = "Montenegro",
  NL = "Netherlands", NO = "Norway", PL = "Poland", PT = "Portugal", RS = "Serbia",
  SK = "Slovakia", SI = "Slovenia", ES = "Spain", SE = "Sweden",
  CH = "Switzerland", UA = "Ukraine", GB = "United Kingdom"
)

cntry_codes <- sort(unique(data$cntry))
cntry_labels <- unname(ifelse(cntry_codes %in% names(country_names),
                               country_names[cntry_codes], cntry_codes))
name_order <- order(cntry_labels)
cntry_codes <- cntry_codes[name_order]
cntry_labels <- cntry_labels[name_order]

groups <- c("All", cntry_codes)
group_labels <- c("All countries", cntry_labels)

ppltrst_by_country <- lapply(groups, function(g) {
  d <- if (g == "All") data else data |> filter(cntry == g)
  d |>
    filter(!is.na(ppltrst)) |>
    count(ppltrst) |>
    mutate(percentage = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 0),
           percentage_precise = sprintf("%.1f", n / sum(n) * 100))
})

avg_scores <- vapply(ppltrst_by_country, function(d) {
  round(weighted.mean(d$ppltrst, w = d$n), 1)
}, numeric(1))

total_n <- vapply(ppltrst_by_country, function(d) sum(d$n), numeric(1))

bar_colors_by_country <- lapply(ppltrst_by_country, function(d) {
  ifelse(d$percentage == max(d$percentage), ess_red_dark, ess_red)
})

pplfair_by_country <- lapply(groups, function(g) {
  d <- if (g == "All") data else data |> filter(cntry == g)
  d |>
    filter(!is.na(pplfair)) |>
    count(pplfair) |>
    mutate(percentage = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 0),
           percentage_precise = sprintf("%.1f", n / sum(n) * 100))
})

avg_scores_fair <- vapply(pplfair_by_country, function(d) {
  round(weighted.mean(d$pplfair, w = d$n), 1)
}, numeric(1))

total_n_fair <- vapply(pplfair_by_country, function(d) sum(d$n), numeric(1))

bar_colors_by_country_fair <- lapply(pplfair_by_country, function(d) {
  ifelse(d$percentage == max(d$percentage), ess_red_dark, ess_red)
})
```

## Row

```{r}
#| label: ppltrst-bar
initial <- ppltrst_by_country[[1]]

avg_annotation <- function(avg) {
  list(
    text = paste0("Average trust level: <span style='color:#1a1a1a;font-weight:700;'>", avg, "</span>"),
    xref = "paper", x = 1, xanchor = "right",
    yref = "paper", y = 1.02, yanchor = "top",
    align = "right",
    showarrow = FALSE,
    font = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12, color = "#555555")
  )
}

n_annotation <- function(n) {
  list(
    text = paste0("n: <span style='color:#1a1a1a;font-weight:700;'>",
                  formatC(n, big.mark = " ", format = "d"), "</span>"),
    xref = "paper", x = 1, xanchor = "right",
    yref = "paper", y = 0.96, yanchor = "top",
    align = "right",
    showarrow = FALSE,
    font = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12, color = "#555555")
  )
}

avg_annotation_fair <- function(avg) {
  list(
    text = paste0("Average fairness level: <span style='color:#1a1a1a;font-weight:700;'>", avg, "</span>"),
    xref = "paper", x = 1, xanchor = "right",
    yref = "paper", y = 1.02, yanchor = "top",
    align = "right",
    showarrow = FALSE,
    font = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12, color = "#555555")
  )
}

buttons <- lapply(seq_along(groups), function(i) {
  d <- ppltrst_by_country[[i]]
  list(
    method = "update",
    args = list(
      list(
        x = list(I(as.character(d$ppltrst))),
        y = list(I(d$percentage)),
        text = list(I(paste0("<b>", d$percentage, "%</b>"))),
        hovertext = list(I(paste0("<b>", formatC(d$n, big.mark = " ", format = "d"), "</b> respondents"))),
        "marker.color" = list(I(bar_colors_by_country[[i]]))
      ),
      list(
        annotations = list(avg_annotation(avg_scores[i]), n_annotation(total_n[i]))
      )
    ),
    label = group_labels[i]
  )
})

plot_ly(elementId = "ppltrst-bar") |>
  add_trace(
    data = initial,
    x = ~factor(ppltrst, levels = 0:10),
    y = ~percentage,
    type = "bar",
    marker = list(color = bar_colors_by_country[[1]]),
    text = ~paste0("<b>", percentage, "%</b>"),
    textposition = "outside",
    textfont = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 16, color = "#1a1a1a"),
    hovertext = ~paste0("<b>", formatC(n, big.mark = " ", format = "d"), "</b> respondents"),
    hoverinfo = "text"
  ) |>
  layout(
    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "<b>Most People Can Be Trusted</b> ",
        "<span style='color:#666666;font-weight:400;'>(0: You can't be too careful, 10: Most people can be trusted)</span>"
      ),
      font = title_font,
      x = 0
    ),
    annotations = list(avg_annotation(avg_scores[1]), n_annotation(total_n[1])),
    updatemenus = list(list(
      type = "dropdown",
      buttons = buttons,
      x = 1,
      y = 1.2,
      xanchor = "right",
      bgcolor = "#ffffff",
      bordercolor = "#dddddd",
      borderwidth = 1,
      pad = list(l = 12, r = 12, t = 6, b = 6),
      font = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12, color = "#333333")
    )),
    showlegend = FALSE,
    dragmode = FALSE,
    hoverlabel = list(
      bgcolor = ess_red_hover,
      bordercolor = ess_red_hover,
      font = list(color = "#ffffff", family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 13)
    ),
    xaxis = list(title = list(text = "Trust level (0 - 10)",
                              font = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12, color = "#555555")),
                 showgrid = FALSE, showline = FALSE, zeroline = FALSE,
                 tickmode = "linear", dtick = 1,
                 tickfont = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12)),
    yaxis = list(title = "", showgrid = FALSE, showline = FALSE,
                 zeroline = FALSE, tickfont = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 13),
                 ticksuffix = "%",
                 range = c(0, 40)),
    paper_bgcolor = "white",
    plot_bgcolor = "white"
  ) |>
  config(displayModeBar = FALSE)
```

## Row

```{r}
#| label: pplfair-bar
initial_fair <- pplfair_by_country[[1]]

buttons_fair <- lapply(seq_along(groups), function(i) {
  d <- pplfair_by_country[[i]]
  list(
    method = "update",
    args = list(
      list(
        x = list(I(as.character(d$pplfair))),
        y = list(I(d$percentage)),
        text = list(I(paste0("<b>", d$percentage, "%</b>"))),
        hovertext = list(I(paste0("<b>", formatC(d$n, big.mark = " ", format = "d"), "</b> respondents"))),
        "marker.color" = list(I(bar_colors_by_country_fair[[i]]))
      ),
      list(
        annotations = list(avg_annotation_fair(avg_scores_fair[i]), n_annotation(total_n_fair[i]))
      )
    ),
    label = group_labels[i]
  )
})

plot_ly(elementId = "pplfair-bar") |>
  add_trace(
    data = initial_fair,
    x = ~factor(pplfair, levels = 0:10),
    y = ~percentage,
    type = "bar",
    marker = list(color = bar_colors_by_country_fair[[1]]),
    text = ~paste0("<b>", percentage, "%</b>"),
    textposition = "outside",
    textfont = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 16, color = "#1a1a1a"),
    hovertext = ~paste0("<b>", formatC(n, big.mark = " ", format = "d"), "</b> respondents"),
    hoverinfo = "text"
  ) |>
  layout(
    title = list(
      text = paste0(
        "<b>Most People Try to Be Fair</b> ",
        "<span style='color:#666666;font-weight:400;'>(0: Most people try to take advantage of me, 10: Most people try to be fair)</span>"
      ),
      font = title_font,
      x = 0
    ),
    annotations = list(avg_annotation_fair(avg_scores_fair[1]), n_annotation(total_n_fair[1])),
    updatemenus = list(list(
      type = "dropdown",
      buttons = buttons_fair,
      x = 1,
      y = 1.2,
      xanchor = "right",
      bgcolor = "#ffffff",
      bordercolor = "#dddddd",
      borderwidth = 1,
      pad = list(l = 12, r = 12, t = 6, b = 6),
      font = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12, color = "#333333")
    )),
    showlegend = FALSE,
    dragmode = FALSE,
    hoverlabel = list(
      bgcolor = ess_red_hover,
      bordercolor = ess_red_hover,
      font = list(color = "#ffffff", family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 13)
    ),
    xaxis = list(title = list(text = "Fairness level (0 - 10)",
                              font = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12, color = "#555555")),
                 showgrid = FALSE, showline = FALSE, zeroline = FALSE,
                 tickmode = "linear", dtick = 1,
                 tickfont = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 12)),
    yaxis = list(title = "", showgrid = FALSE, showline = FALSE,
                 zeroline = FALSE, tickfont = list(family = "Inter, sans-serif", size = 13),
                 ticksuffix = "%",
                 range = c(0, 40)),
    paper_bgcolor = "white",
    plot_bgcolor = "white"
  ) |>
  config(displayModeBar = FALSE)
```

## Column

```{r}
#| label: footer
#| output: asis
cat('
<style>
.ess-filter-select {
  font-family: Inter, sans-serif;
  font-size: 12px;
  color: #333333;
  background-color: #ffffff;
  border: 1px solid #dddddd;
  border-radius: 4px;
  padding: 4px 10px;
  cursor: pointer;
}
</style>

<script>
// Plotly\'s built-in dropdown (updatemenus) renders its scrollable list in
// SVG, and its wheel-scroll handling does not reliably stop the event from
// bubbling up to the page - so scrolling inside the list also scrolls the
// whole dashboard. Native <select> elements do not have this problem, so we
// build one from the same button/args data Plotly already has, wire it to
// call the identical Plotly.update(), and hide the original dropdown.
function essReplaceDropdown(id) {
  var gd = document.getElementById(id);
  if (!gd || !gd.layout || !gd.layout.updatemenus || gd.dataset.essFilterReady) return;
  gd.dataset.essFilterReady = "1";

  var buttons = gd.layout.updatemenus[0].buttons;

  var select = document.createElement("select");
  select.className = "ess-filter-select";
  buttons.forEach(function(btn, i) {
    var opt = document.createElement("option");
    opt.value = i;
    opt.textContent = btn.label;
    select.appendChild(opt);
  });

  select.addEventListener("change", function() {
    var btn = buttons[select.selectedIndex];
    Plotly.update(gd, btn.args[0], btn.args[1]);
  });

  var wrapper = document.createElement("div");
  wrapper.style.textAlign = "right";
  wrapper.style.marginBottom = "4px";
  wrapper.appendChild(select);

  gd.parentElement.insertBefore(wrapper, gd);
  Plotly.relayout(gd, {"updatemenus[0].visible": false});
}

document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function() {
  var ids = ["ppltrst-bar", "pplfair-bar"];
  var interval = setInterval(function() {
    var allDone = true;
    ids.forEach(function(id) {
      var gd = document.getElementById(id);
      if (gd && gd.layout && gd.layout.updatemenus) {
        essReplaceDropdown(id);
      }
      if (!gd || !gd.dataset.essFilterReady) allDone = false;
    });
    if (allDone) clearInterval(interval);
  }, 200);
  setTimeout(function() { clearInterval(interval); }, 10000);
});
</script>

<p style="font-size: 12px; color: #555555; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; text-align: right; padding: 0.5rem 1rem;">
Data: <a href="https://https://ess.sikt.no/en/" target="_blank" style="color: #555555; text-decoration: underline;">The European Social Survey, Round 11 (2023/24)</a>
</p>')
```
